name: Crouching Lion Trail
tagline: Short but steep scramble to a dramatic rock formation with panoramic views of Kahana Bay and the Ko'olau range.
parking: Small pullout on Kamehameha Hwy near mile marker 27. Limited to 5-6 cars
bestTime: Early morning for cooler temps and better light. Trail gets full sun with no shade
talkStory: A short (~1 mile) but intense hike with steep, muddy sections and some rock scrambling near the top. The payoff is incredible — 360-degree views of Kahana Bay, the Ko'olau mountains, and the Windward Coast. The 'Crouching Lion' is a rock formation at the summit that resembles a lion in repose. The trail is not officially maintained and can be slippery when wet — wear shoes with good grip, not slippers. Not recommended for young kids due to steep drop-offs near the top. Trailhead can be hard to find — look for the worn path on the mauka (mountain) side of the highway across from Kahana Bay.
